sections.paragraph:
φυτεία, ας, ἡ (X., Theophr. et al.; pap [e.g. POxy 1778, 31], LXX; PsSol 14:4; GrBar 4:8; AscIs 4:3 and Jos., Ant. 3, 281 in the sense ‘planting’) that which is planted, a plant (Aelian, VH 3, 40; Athen. 5, 207d; OGI 606, 7; Philo, Op. M. 41) in imagery (PsSol 14:4) Mt 15:13; ITr 11:1; IPhld 3:1 (w. Ign. cp. Synes., Prov. 10 p. 100d of the truly good man: ἔστιν ἐπὶ γῆς φυτὸν οὐράνιον after Pla., Tim. 90a).—M-M.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Ign. Ign. = Ignatius; always simply I. when cited with one of his epistles

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Synes Synes , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1070
